title: Assertion failure in `insertdict`
author: devdanzin
body:
# Crash report

### What happened?

It's possible to cause the interpreter to abort by running the code below:

```python
from threading import Thread
from asyncio import Runner, gather, to_thread

class Str(str): ...
class Class: ...

def instance_1__setattr__():
    Class().__setattr__(Str(), "")

async def run_async_tasks():
    await gather(to_thread(instance_1__setattr__))

Thread(target=Class().__setattr__, args=("", "")).start()
Runner().run(run_async_tasks())
```

Backtrace:

May be related to https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142731.

Found using [fusil](https://github.com/devdanzin/fusil) by @vstinner.


### CPython versions tested on:

CPython main branch

### Operating systems tested on:

Linux

### Output from running 'python -VV' on the command line:

Python 3.15.0a3+ free-threading build (heads/main:cf6758ff9eb, Dec 24 2025, 21:09:14) [Clang 21.1.2 (2ubuntu6)]

<!-- gh-linked-prs -->
### Linked PRs
* gh-143285
* gh-143771
* gh-143772
<!-- /gh-linked-prs -->

comments.author: colesbury
comments.body:
Not free threading specific. The assertion is just wrong after:
* https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/140558

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body: Should we just remove it, or is there an interesting check to assert there?

comments.author: colesbury
comments.body:
I think it's more than just the assertion that is wrong. @methane I think the logic in the PR we reviewed doesn't handle the case where you insert a unicode subclass that matches something in the shared keys (but not in the dict) properly. Here is a repro without any threads:

```python
class MyClass: pass
class MyStr(str): pass

obj = MyClass()
obj.attr1 = "attr1"

obj2 = MyClass()
d = obj2.__dict__
d[MyStr("attr1")] = "attr1"  # Crashes in debug builds
assert isinstance(list(d.keys())[0], MyStr)  # Fails in release builds of 3.13+ with "IndexError: list index out of range"
```

comments.author: methane
methane
comments.body:
For the record, this issue is very similar to #142218.

#142218 is crash when inserting non-Unicode key when matching Unicode key is in split table and there is a value.
This issue is crash when inserting non-Unicode key when matching Unicode key is in split table, but there is no value in the dict.
